Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The logs show a set of cases with common steps order received OR material sourcing MS quality screening QS assembly AS in line quality check IQC test TST re testing RT packaging PK labeling LB distribution planning DP dispatch D Some cases skip labeling Some cases have multiple repetitions of IQC and AS combos some have multiple repetitions of RT and TST combos

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We also have optional labeling after packaging maybe labeling is optional so we need a XOR after PK to decide LB or skip

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We have two loops one loop for AS and IQC the quality loop and one loop for TST and RT the test loop

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The pattern appears AS then IQC then AS then IQC then AS then IQC After final IQC they go to TST So it is like multiple cycles

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The loop semantics is execute A then either exit or execute B then A again So for a loop that repeats AS and IQC we can define A as AS and B as IQC In this case after AS you either exit that is go to TST or execute IQC then AS again

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 they have AS then TST skipping IQC That corresponds to exit after first AS Good

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For the test loop the pattern is after TST either exit if passes or do RT then TST again So we can define loop children TST and RT

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 TST RT TST RT TST So loop can handle that

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The sequence of major steps is OR then MS then QS then quality loop then test loop then PK then labeling choice then DP then D

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: There is no explicit concurrency however we can still define a StrictPartialOrder that defines the ordering constraints

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus we can define a StrictPartialOrder with nodes being the major components OR MS QS quality loop test loop PK labeling choice DP D The order edges are OR to MS MS to QS QS to quality loop quality loop to test loop test loop to PK PK to labeling choice labeling choice to DP DP to D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 corresponds to OR MS QS quality loop with one iteration of IQC then exit test loop with one iteration of RT then exit PK labeling DP D Case 2 similar but labeling skipped Case 3 loop with multiple IQC and AS repetitions Case 4 skip IQC and skip RT Case 5 multiple repetitions in both loops
